text:
To solve this problem, we need to understand the pattern of the light bulbs being turned on and off. 

In round 1, every 1st bulb is toggled, which means all 100 bulbs are turned on.
In round 2, every 2nd bulb is toggled, which means bulbs 2, 4, 6, ..., 100 are turned off.
In round 3, every 3rd bulb is toggled, which means bulbs 3, 6, 9, ..., 99 are turned on or off depending on their current state.
This process continues until round 100.

A bulb will be toggled an odd number of times if and only if it has an odd number of factors. This is because each factor of the bulb's position represents a round in which the bulb is toggled. 

A number has an odd number of factors if and only if it is a perfect square. This is because factors come in pairs, except when the number is a perfect square, in which case one of the factors is repeated.

So, the bulbs that will be on after all rounds are the perfect squares: 1, 4, 9, 16, ..., 100. 

There are 10 perfect squares between 1 and 100 (inclusive).

The final answer is 10.
